core_case.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 21?
core_case.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 21?
core_case.contract_ambiguity_reason: Rules are explicit, but settlement depends on the exact Binance noon-ET 1-minute close surface rather than a broader benchmark.
core_case.freshness_driver: A short six-day window means any new macro or crypto shock and the near-resolution Binance cushion can move the estimate materially.
decision_blockers: No strong independently verified edge versus market after synthesis.
Short-horizon BTC volatility can erase the current cushion before the exact settlement minute.
Single-minute Binance-specific settlement creates operational and path dependence near threshold.

structured_handoff_primary.contract_ambiguity_reason: Rules are explicit, but settlement depends on the exact Binance noon-ET 1-minute close surface rather than a broader benchmark.
independently_verified_points: Binance BTCUSDT spot was independently rechecked around 74848 on 2026-04-15, leaving roughly a 4% cushion above 72000.
Recent Binance daily closes were mostly above 72000, so the strike sits inside the current trading regime rather than above it.
The Fed calendar confirms the next scheduled FOMC meeting is April 28-29, after settlement, removing one obvious scheduled macro catalyst from the window.
Contract mechanics consistently point to the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle final Close with a strict greater-than 72000 threshold.
structured_handoff_primary.verification_gap_summary: The main unverified gap is the true probability that a routine 4% downside move or noon-minute microstructure wobble hits before settlement.
structured_handoff_primary.best_countercase_summary: A normal crypto pullback or bad-timed intraday dip can still push the exact Binance settlement minute to 72000 or lower despite an otherwise healthy BTC tape.
structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: Most disagreement is about how much to discount for exact-minute settlement fragility versus the current 4% cushion.
structured_handoff_primary.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if Binance BTC/USDT’s 12:00 ET April 21 one-minute candle final Close is strictly above 72000.
structured_handoff_primary.freshness_driver: A short six-day window means any new macro or crypto shock and the near-resolution Binance cushion can move the estimate materially.

Short-horizon BTC volatility can erase the current cushion before the exact settlement minute.
Single-minute Binance-specific settlement creates operational and path dependence near threshold.

prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

BTC being above 72,000 on the relevant April 21 Binance noon-ET minute remains more likely than not, but the strongest synthesis view is only modestly below the market rather than strongly bearish versus it: spot was independently rechecked near 74.85k with recent daily closes mostly above 72k, and the key surviving risk is not directional BTC weakness alone but the contract’s single-minute, single-venue settlement fragility.

## Why this may matter now

Market implied is 0.78; my synthesized range is 0.72-0.78. That makes any edge versus market marginal to unclear rather than actionable. The only plausible mispricing is that the market may slightly underweight exact-minute Binance settlement fragility, but independent verification was not strong enough to support a large below-market stance.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

The provisional swarm center was about 0.72. I end slightly above that center and with a tighter range because synthesis-stage verification supported the bullish base case more than the most cautious lanes implied: Binance spot was independently rechecked near 74.85k, recent daily closes mostly held above 72k, and the next scheduled FOMC mee …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market's high Yes price is broadly justified by BTC trading materially above 72,000 on the named settlement venue, though exact-minute settlement makes me slightly less bullish than market.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket prices the 72,000 line around 78-81% Yes.
Direct Binance venue data shows BTC already near 74.85k, about 3.96% above threshold.
Given the short horizon, the market's persistence logic is mostly reasonable.
But exact-minute, single-venue settlement adds enough tail risk to justify a slightly lower estimate than market.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was 74850.96 at review time, materially above the 72000 threshold.
Recent Binance 1-minute klines also sat above 74700, so the cushion was not a stale single print.
strongest_disconfirmers: A roughly 4% BTC downside move over six days is plausible.
Resolution depends on one exact Binance minute close, so temporary weakness at the wrong moment can flip the outcome.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much downside volatility the market is implicitly pricing over the remaining window.
Whether any near-term catalyst could cause a sudden BTC risk-off move before April 21 noon ET.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if Binance BTCUSDT loses most of the cushion or volatility spikes; I would move higher if BTC holds comfortably above 72,000 with stable venue behavior into settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Settlement is Tuesday April 21, 2026 at 12:00 PM EDT on the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close, so current cushion matters but can still be eroded over six days.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-quality direct venue data plus explicit contract rules made source-of-truth ambiguity low, though evidence independence was only medium.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is already materially above 72,000 and the known catalyst calendar before April 21 noon ET looks light, so YES is more likely unless an unscheduled macro or crypto-specific downside shock forces a fast drawdown.
main_logical_chain: The contract settles on Binance BTC/USDT at one exact minute, so exact timing and venue mechanics matter.
Current Binance spot is comfortably above 72,000 and recent daily regime has mostly held above that level.
There is no scheduled FOMC decision before settlement, so the main path to NO is an unscheduled macro or crypto-specific risk-off shock.
Absent such a shock, time decay favors YES in a short hold-above-threshold market.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was about 74,830 on April 15, roughly 3.8% above threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes were mostly above 72,000.
strongest_disconfirmers: A routine ~4% BTC downside move before Tuesday noon ET would be enough to settle NO.
The contract resolves on one exact Binance one-minute close, creating path fragility.
unresolved_ambiguities: No direct read on ETF-flow or other near-term crypto-specific catalysts inside the window was obtained.
Unscheduled macro headlines remain inherently hard to pre-verify.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would lower the estimate if BTC quickly lost 74k and 73k, if a major macro shock or crypto-specific stress event emerged, or if Binance-specific settlement-quality concerns increased.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: This is a short-window persistence case: the crucial question is whether any catalyst before April 21 noon ET can force BTC back below 72,000, not whether BTC has long-run upside.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Source quality is medium-high overall: contract mechanics are explicit and Binance is the stated settlement source, while macro timing was cross-checked independently with the Fed calendar; independence is only medium because core evidence is necessarily Binance/Polymarket-centric.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is currently above the threshold and the contract wording is clear, so Yes is more likely than No, but the market is somewhat overconfident because settlement depends on one exact Binance minute close on April 21 at noon ET.
main_logical_chain: Check the exact contract mechanics and verify what counts for settlement.
Verify current Binance BTCUSDT context relative to the 72000 threshold.
Recognize that current spot above threshold supports a Yes lean.
Discount confidence because the market settles on one exact minute close several days away.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance BTCUSDT spot-context check during the run was around 74857, leaving a meaningful cushion over 72000.
Polymarket rules make the settlement source and mechanics explicit, reducing wording ambiguity.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC only needs to fall roughly 3.8% from the checked level to finish at or below 72000, which is plausible over six days in crypto.
The contract settles on a single Binance minute close, so exact-minute timing risk is material.
unresolved_ambiguities: No broad independent macro/context source was successfully retrieved in this run.
Near-term volatility catalysts before April 21 remain unresolved.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move toward the market if BTC holds comfortably above 75000 into April 20-21, and further away if BTC compresses toward 72000-73000 or downside volatility rises.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Timing is central because the contract resolves on the Binance BTCUSDT 1-minute candle close at 12:00 ET on April 21, not on an average or end-of-day price.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High quality for contract interpretation and exchange-specific context, but only medium-low independence because the evidence set is tightly linked to the same mechanism.
